ucs.title: [LLM] Inception / Cloud Atlas remote-template injection chain — Office fetches RTF/DOTM then spawns mshta/cscript/regsvr32
ucs.description: Hunts the Cloud Atlas (G0100) signature delivery chain: a spear-phished Office document (T1566.001) pulls a remote template from an attacker-controlled HTTP endpoint (T1221) and the Office host process spawns mshta.exe / wscript.exe / cscript.exe / regsvr32.exe / EQNEDT32.exe (T1218.005, T1218.010, T1203, T1059.005) within a short window. Cloud Atlas has used this exact RTF-template-injection-into-VBShower/PowerShower handoff continuously since 2018 — winword.exe doing an HTTPS GET for a doc/dotm/xml followed seconds later by an mshta/cscript child is highly distinctive.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","regsvr32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","eqnedt32.exe") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename firstTime as childFirstTime lastTime as childLastTime | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as fetchFirstTime from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") AND All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" by host All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.url | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | where match(url,"(?i)\.(docx?|dotm?|rtf|xls[mx]?|ppt[mx]?|xml)(\?|$)") OR match(url,"(?i)(cloudme|yandex|opendrive|carbonbox)") | rename app as office_app dest as remote_host url as remote_url ] | eval delaySec = childFirstTime - fetchFirstTime | where delaySec >= 0 AND delaySec <= 300 | table childFirstTime fetchFirstTime delaySec host user office_app remote_host remote_url parent_process_name process_name process | sort - childFirstTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let CorrelationWindowSec = 300;
let OfficeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let StagingChildren = dynamic(["mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","regsvr32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
// 1. Office host doing an outbound HTTP fetch of a template-shaped resource
let TemplateFetches = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeApps)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
    | where RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)\.(docx?|dotm?|rtf|xls[mx]?|ppt[mx]?|xml)(\?|$)"
         or RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)(cloudme\.com|yandex|opendrive|carbonbox)"
    | project FetchTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              OfficeBin   = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              FetchUrl    = RemoteUrl, FetchIP = RemoteIP;
// 2. Office spawns a known-lure interpreter within the correlation window
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeApps)
| where FileName in~ (StagingChildren)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner TemplateFetches on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (FetchTime .. FetchTime + CorrelationWindowSec * 1s)
| project FetchTime, ChildTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, FetchTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          OfficeBin, FetchUrl, FetchIP,
          ChildBin = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by ChildTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let CorrelationWindowSec = 300;
let OfficeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let StagingChildren = dynamic(["mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","regsvr32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
// Network fetches by Office processes (vendor-flexible via ASIM)
let TemplateFetches = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActingProcessName has_any (OfficeApps) or tostring(parse_json(tostring(parse_json(EventOriginalUid)))) has_any (OfficeApps)
    | where isnotempty(Url)
    | where Url matches regex @"(?i)\.(docx?|dotm?|rtf|xls[mx]?|ppt[mx]?|xml)(\?|$)"
         or Url matches regex @"(?i)(cloudme\.com|yandex|opendrive|carbonbox)"
    | project FetchTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, FetchUrl = Url, FetchIP = DstIpAddr;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ParentProcessName has_any (OfficeApps)
| where TargetProcessName has_any (StagingChildren)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner TemplateFetches on DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (FetchTime .. FetchTime + CorrelationWindowSec * 1s)
| project FetchTime, ChildTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, FetchTime),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ParentProcessName, FetchUrl, FetchIP,
          TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessCommandLine
| order by ChildTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Inception / Cloud Atlas Office-to-LOLBin Spawn Following Remote Template Fetch
id: 8fbc3a82-7c1e-4b5d-9a1c-c0a710a59001
status: experimental
description: Detects the Cloud Atlas (G0100) staged-execution pattern where an Office product spawns mshta, regsvr32, wscript, cscript, powershell, or eqnedt32 — the canonical handoff after a remote-template-injection lure.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0100/
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/unit42-inception-attackers-target-europe-year-old-office-vulnerability/
  - https://securelist.com/recent-cloud-atlas-activity/92016/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1221
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.g0100
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Legacy line-of-business macros that legitimately invoke wscript/cscript helpers
  - Software-deployment scripts launched from Outlook calendar mail-merge templates
level: high
ucs.rationale: Cloud Atlas is the originator and most prolific user of the RTF/DOCX-remote-template-injection → VBShower/PowerShower chain — Kaspersky, Unit 42 and Symantec have all documented this exact handoff (winword fetches a remote template, EQNEDT32 or wscript spawns, then PowerShower stages). Generic phishing actors typically deliver macros that spawn powershell directly; the *combination* of (Office → o

ucs.title: [LLM] Inception / Cloud Atlas LOLBin C2 to legitimate cloud-storage providers (CloudMe, Yandex Disk, OpenDrive)
ucs.description: Hunts Cloud Atlas's namesake C2 channel: PowerShower, VBShower, and the newer PowerShellRunner backdoors check in to legitimate WebDAV/cloud-storage providers (CloudMe, Yandex Disk, OpenDrive, Mail.ru cloud) over HTTPS to retrieve tasking and exfiltrate data (T1102 + T1071.001 + T1573.001). The high-fidelity pivot is a Windows scripting/LOLBin process — mshta.exe, wscript.exe, cscript.exe, powershell.exe, regsvr32.exe — making outbound HTTPS to one of these consumer cloud-storage endpoints from a corporate workstation that has no legitimate reason to use them.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.url) as urls dc(All_Traffic.url) as urlCount sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytesOut sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as bytesIn from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","regsvr32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe") AND (All_Traffic.dest_host IN ("*cloudme.com","webdav.cloudme.com","*.yandex.net","*disk.yandex.*","webdav.yandex.ru","*.opendrive.com","*cloud.mail.ru","webdav.cloud.mail.ru","*fex.net","*carbonbox.org") OR match(All_Traffic.url,"(?i)(cloudme|webdav\.yandex|disk\.yandex|opendrive\.com|cloud\.mail\.ru|fex\.net|carbonbox)")) by host All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | where urlCount > 1 OR bytesOut > 50000 | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let CloudC2Hosts = dynamic([
    "cloudme.com","webdav.cloudme.com",
    "yandex.net","yandex.ru","disk.yandex","webdav.yandex.ru",
    "opendrive.com",
    "cloud.mail.ru","webdav.cloud.mail.ru",
    "fex.net","carbonbox"
]);
let ScriptingBins = dynamic([
    "mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","regsvr32.exe",
    "powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe"
]);
// 30-day per-host baseline of cloud-storage usage so we only alert on first-seen pairs
let Baseline = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(30d) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ScriptingBins)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (CloudC2Hosts)
    | summarize by DeviceId, RemoteUrl;
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ScriptingBins)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where RemoteUrl has_any (CloudC2Hosts)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on DeviceId, RemoteUrl
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1m)),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            Urls      = make_set(RemoteUrl, 25)
            by DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
               InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| where ConnCount >= 3 or DistinctMinutes >= 2   // beacon-ish or sustained
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let CloudC2Hosts = dynamic([
    "cloudme.com","webdav.cloudme.com",
    "yandex.net","yandex.ru","disk.yandex","webdav.yandex.ru",
    "opendrive.com",
    "cloud.mail.ru","webdav.cloud.mail.ru",
    "fex.net","carbonbox"
]);
let ScriptingBins = dynamic([
    "mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","regsvr32.exe",
    "powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe"
]);
let Baseline = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | where ActingProcessName has_any (ScriptingBins)
    | where Url has_any (CloudC2Hosts) or DstHostname has_any (CloudC2Hosts)
    | summarize by DvcHostname, Url;
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActingProcessName has_any (ScriptingBins)
| where (Url has_any (CloudC2Hosts) or DstHostname has_any (CloudC2Hosts))
| where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on DvcHostname, Url
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            SampleCmd = any(ActingProcessCommandLine),
            Urls = make_set(Url, 25)
            by DvcHostname, SrcUsername, ActingProcessName, DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber
| where ConnCount >= 3 or DistinctMinutes >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Cloud Atlas earned its CrowdStrike/Kaspersky cluster name precisely because of this technique — they were among the first APTs to systematically use CloudMe (WebDAV) and Yandex Disk as their primary C2 channels for the PowerShower / VBShower implants, documented in Kaspersky's 2014 and 2019 reports and Symantec's 2018 Inception Framework write-up. A corporate workstation invoking mshta/wscript/reg

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
